questions.question_text: Under Arkansas Code 17-42-316, agents owe clients a duty of:
distractors: Reasonable care only
Limited loyalty
Best efforts when convenient
questions.explanation: Arkansas law requires agents to exercise 'absolute fidelity' to protect and promote their client's interests. This is a high standard codified in §17-42-316.

questions.question_text: A listing agent receives a lowball offer. Under the duty of absolute fidelity, the agent must:
questions.correct_answer: Present the offer to the seller promptly
distractors: Reject the offer on the seller's behalf
Negotiate a higher price before presenting
Wait for a better offer before presenting
questions.explanation: The duty of absolute fidelity includes presenting all offers to the client. Agents cannot withhold or screen offers — that decision belongs to the client.

questions.question_text: Which duty requires an agent to recommend their client seek a lawyer or inspector when needed?
questions.correct_answer: Duty to advise client to obtain expert advice
distractors: Duty of confidentiality
Duty to follow lawful instructions
Duty to exercise reasonable skill
questions.explanation: Among the duties of absolute fidelity, agents must advise clients to obtain expert advice when appropriate — such as recommending legal counsel or home inspections.

questions.question_text: An agent learns their client plans to use the property for an illegal purpose. Must the agent follow this instruction?
questions.correct_answer: No, agents only follow lawful instructions
distractors: Yes, the duty of fidelity requires compliance
Yes, if it is in writing
Only if the broker approves
questions.explanation: The duty of absolute fidelity requires following lawful instructions of the client. Agents are not obligated — and should refuse — to follow instructions that involve illegal activity.

questions.question_text: The duty to account for all money and property received means an agent must:
questions.correct_answer: Track and report all funds handled
distractors: Invest client funds for maximum return
Commingle funds with personal accounts
Withhold earnest money until closing
questions.explanation: Agents must account for all money and property received in connection with a transaction. This includes proper documentation, reporting, and never commingling funds.

questions.question_text: Under Arkansas law, the duty of honest dealing extends to:
distractors: Only the agent's client
Only licensed parties
Only the party paying commission
questions.explanation: While fiduciary duties are owed to the client, the duty of honest dealing extends to ALL parties in the transaction, including non-clients. Agents cannot misrepresent facts to anyone.

questions.question_text: A buyer's agent knows the property has foundation issues that the seller hasn't disclosed. The agent must:
questions.correct_answer: Disclose the known defect to the buyer
distractors: Keep silent to avoid interfering with the sale
Only disclose if the buyer asks specifically
Defer to the seller's disclosure obligations
questions.explanation: Agents must disclose material facts known to them to all parties. The duty of honest dealing requires truthfulness regardless of which party the agent represents.

questions.question_text: A seller's agent is asked by a prospective buyer about neighborhood noise. The agent knows of a nearby construction project. What should the agent do?
questions.correct_answer: Disclose the known construction project honestly
distractors: Remain silent since the buyer is not a client
Say only positive things about the neighborhood
Refer the question to the seller directly
questions.explanation: Honest dealing requires truthful responses to all parties. Even though the buyer is not the agent's client, the agent cannot misrepresent or omit known material facts.

questions.question_text: Which statement BEST distinguishes honest dealing from absolute fidelity?
questions.correct_answer: Fidelity is owed to clients; honest dealing to all
distractors: They apply to the same parties identically
Honest dealing only applies to written statements
Fidelity applies only during active negotiations
questions.explanation: Absolute fidelity (loyalty, confidentiality, advocacy) is owed only to clients. Honest dealing (truthfulness, no misrepresentation) is owed to all parties in the transaction.

questions.question_text: An agent intentionally misstates the square footage of a home to a non-client buyer. This violates:
questions.correct_answer: The duty of honest dealing with all parties
distractors: Only the advertising rules
No duty, since the buyer is not a client
The duty of confidentiality to the seller
questions.explanation: Intentionally misrepresenting material facts violates the duty of honest dealing, which extends to all parties — not just clients. Misrepresentation can also constitute fraud.

questions.question_text: Can an Arkansas client waive the agent's core duties under §17-42-316?
distractors: Yes, with written consent
Yes, if the broker approves
Only for commercial transactions
questions.explanation: Core duties under Arkansas Code §17-42-316 are non-waivable. Clients cannot agree to waive an agent's duty of fidelity, honest dealing, or other statutory obligations.

questions.question_text: Which of the following CAN be modified by written agreement in Arkansas?
distractors: The duty to disclose material facts
The duty of honest dealing
The duty to account for funds
questions.explanation: While core duties are non-waivable, the scope of services may be modified by written agreement. For example, a limited-service listing can define specific services to be provided.

questions.question_text: A seller asks their agent to keep a known roof leak secret from buyers. The agent should:
questions.correct_answer: Refuse, as material fact disclosure is non-waivable
distractors: Comply with the client's lawful instruction
Keep silent unless directly asked by the buyer
Disclose only after the contract is signed
questions.explanation: The duty to disclose material facts is non-waivable. Even though agents must follow client instructions, they cannot conceal known material defects regardless of client wishes.

questions.question_text: After a transaction closes, is a former client's confidential information still protected?
distractors: No, duties end when the transaction closes
Only for 3 years after closing
Only if a written agreement says so
questions.explanation: An agent's duty to keep confidential information confidential continues after the agency relationship ends. This is part of the non-waivable duties under §17-42-316.

questions.question_text: The distinction between waivable and non-waivable duties in Arkansas means that:
questions.correct_answer: Certain protections exist regardless of contract terms
distractors: All duties can be negotiated away in writing
Only broker duties are non-waivable
Non-waivable duties apply only to dual agency
questions.explanation: Non-waivable duties ensure minimum protections for consumers that cannot be contracted away. Even limited-service agreements cannot eliminate core statutory duties.
